_comment: IQUEST Question Schema v1 — Claude follows this when generating questions for !IQUEST
_usage: Fetch IQUEST_Shell.jsx, replace GUIDE_TITLE / STORAGE_KEY / QUESTIONS array, output as JSX artifact

id.description: Sequential integer starting at 1. Follow-up questions generated by Claude API use ids 201+

tag.description: Uppercase shortcode for this question. Prefix with a topic abbreviation, e.g. 'FD-TRIGGER', 'LISP-SCOPE', 'QTS-EXPORT'. Max 20 chars. Used in summary output.

color.description: Accent color for this question's tag badge and selected-option highlight.
color.guidance: Rotate through colors across questions. Avoid repeating adjacent colors.

q.description: The question text. Should be a single clear decision question. Keep under 120 chars. No trailing question mark in tag.

ctx.description: 2-3 sentences of context: why this decision matters, what it affects downstream, and what Ben should consider when answering. This is shown expanded under the question.

rec.description: Claude's recommendation. Must match an option value (or array of values for multi). A rec is always required — if genuinely uncertain, pick the safest or most reversible option.

val.description: Value matching an opt.v. For multi type, an array of values.

why.description: Optional 1-sentence rationale shown to Claude on follow-up submit. Not displayed in UI.

type.description: choice = single-select from opts. multi = multiple-select from opts. yn = yes/no (no opts array needed).

opts.required: required for choice and multi types; omit for yn
opts.description: Answer options. Order: recommended option first (or at position that feels natural), then alternatives.

v.description: Value key — snake_case, no spaces. Used internally and in summary output.

l.description: Display label. Short and scannable, under 50 chars.

sub.description: Optional 1-line subtitle shown under the label in smaller text. Good for a quick clarifier.

pros.description: 2-4 short pros for this option. Shown in the REC/SELECTED box when this option is active. Include only when the tradeoff is meaningful enough to surface.

cons.description: 1-3 short cons. Pair with pros — don't include cons without pros.

question_set_rules.count: 3–8 questions per IQUEST. Fewer if decisions are clear; more if scope is genuinely wide.
question_set_rules.ordering: Sequence questions so earlier answers inform later ones (dependencies first).
question_set_rules.scope: Only ask about decisions that are actually blocking implementation. Don't ask about things already decided or inferable from context.
question_set_rules.no_duplicates: If a question was answered in a previous IQUEST session (check summary or prior answers), don't re-ask it.
question_set_rules.rec_required: Every question must have a rec. Claude should have an opinion.
question_set_rules.yn_usage: Use yn for genuine yes/no decisions only. Don't use it to avoid defining options.

inject_instructions.step_2: Replace GUIDE_TITLE constant with a descriptive title, e.g. 'FD Right-Click Ticket System'
inject_instructions.step_3: Replace STORAGE_KEY with a stable key, e.g. 'iquest_fd_tickets_v1' (keep _v1 suffix)
inject_instructions.step_4: Replace the QUESTIONS array with generated question objects following this schema
inject_instructions.step_5: Output the result as a JSX artifact — do not modify any other part of the shell

choice_with_pros_cons.q: How should right-click tickets be triggered on mobile?
choice_with_pros_cons.ctx: The ticket system uses a right-click context menu on desktop. Mobile doesn't have right-click. This determines whether mobile gets ticket support now or is deferred to a later build.

rec.why: Mobile scope adds complexity without strong immediate need — can be added later without rework.

yn_example.q: Should tickets persist across browser sessions?
yn_example.ctx: Tickets created in one session could be saved to localStorage so they survive a refresh. Without persistence, tickets are in-memory only and lost on reload.

multi_example.q: Which ticket export formats should be supported?
multi_example.ctx: Exported tickets need to be usable outside the tool — e.g. pasted into Teams, Outlook, or a text file. The formats affect how much parsing logic is needed.
